project.name: YouTube Research Pipeline
project.description: Automated research pipeline using Claude Code as orchestration layer. Searches YouTube for videos matching semantic research criteria, filters results via Claude as intermediate semantic layer, ingests into NotebookLM for corpus-based research, and produces Obsidian-compatible markdown knowledge base with semantic tags.

stages.name: YouTube Search & Filter
stages.description: Two-phase process. First, YouTube Data API v3 performs keyword-based search and retrieves candidate video metadata. Second, Claude evaluates each candidate's metadata against user-defined research criteria and produces a relevance score and inclusion/exclusion rationale.
stages.rationale: YouTube search ranks by engagement metrics, not research relevance. NotebookLM cannot select its own inputs. Claude bridges the gap as a semantic filter that reads unstructured metadata and evaluates it against natural-language research criteria. This pre-filtering protects NotebookLM's downstream retrieval quality.

inputs.user_query: Natural language description of research topic and criteria

outputs.format: JSON array of video metadata objects

youtube_data_api_v3.credential: API key from Google Cloud project
youtube_data_api_v3.quota: 10,000 units/day. Search=100 units, video details=1 unit, captions list=50 units.

claude_code_max.role: Semantic filtering of metadata against research criteria. Runs as Claude Code's own reasoning within the Max plan allocation.
claude_code_max.auth: Max subscription login. No separate Anthropic API key required. If ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var is set, Claude Code will use API billing instead — unset it to stay on Max plan.
claude_code_max.input: Batch of video metadata objects + research criteria prompt
claude_code_max.output: Same objects with relevance_score, inclusion_rationale, included boolean
claude_code_max.cost: Consumes Max plan usage allocation (shared across claude.ai, Claude Desktop, and Claude Code). No per-token billing.

1A.name: Corpus Editorial Policy Prompt
1A.description: Design reusable prompt template for Claude's semantic filtering step. This is the editorial policy of the research corpus and the highest-leverage design decision in the pipeline.
1A.deliverable: Prompt template with slots for: inclusion criteria, exclusion criteria, quality signals (channel authority, speaker expertise, content depth), scoring rubric (1-5 scale with inclusion threshold), domain-specific configuration (topic, field, key terms, known authoritative channels).
1A.constraint: Must be completed before first pipeline run against real data.

quota_planning.cost_estimate: ~5,200 API units (1 search page=100 + 100 video details=100 + 100 caption checks=5,000)

stages.name: URL Extraction & Staging
stages.description: Write filtered results from Stage 1 to a structured JSON checkpoint file on disk. This file is the input contract for Stage 3 and serves as audit trail and pipeline checkpoint.
stages.rationale: Decouples search/filter from ingestion. Provides inspection point for reviewing what the pipeline selected and why. If Stage 3 fails, Stage 1 does not need to be re-run.

metadata.research_criteria_version: string (hash or version of Subtask 1A prompt used)

stages.name: NotebookLM Ingestion
stages.description: Claude Code connects to NotebookLM via notebooklm-mcp-cli MCP server, creates a notebook for the research project, and batch-ingests YouTube URLs from the Stage 2 JSON file. Failures are logged with video ID and error details.
stages.rationale: MCP path chosen over Python CLI for tighter agentic feedback loop. Claude can react to ingestion failures in real time. Decision logged for re-evaluation if MCP proves fragile.

outputs.notebook: NotebookLM notebook with ingested video sources

notebooklm_mcp_cli.install: pip install notebooklm-mcp-cli OR uv tool install notebooklm-mcp-cli
notebooklm_mcp_cli.caveat: Uses undocumented Google APIs. Can break without notice.

fallback.description: Python CLI providing same capabilities. Produces standalone scripts runnable without Claude Code. Use if MCP path breaks.

error_handling.per_video_failure: Log error, skip video, continue batch. Surface failures in ingestion log for manual review.
error_handling.auth_failure: Halt ingestion. Prompt user to re-authenticate via 'nlm login'.
error_handling.rate_limit: Implement exponential backoff. Log rate limit events.

stages.name: Corpus Query Layer
stages.description: NotebookLM provides persistent semantic index over ingested videos. Cross-source synthesis and grounded citations via conversational queries through MCP.
stages.notes: NotebookLM's retrieval logic is opaque. No control over chunk size, embedding model, or similarity threshold. Acceptable for exploratory research; may limit systematic coverage verification.

stages.name: Deep Research
stages.description: Structured investigation of specific topics against the corpus. Extract claims, arguments, and evidence with source attribution.

stages.name: Knowledge Base Output
stages.description: Synthesize research outputs into Obsidian-compatible .md files with standardized YAML frontmatter.

stages.name: Semantic Tagging
stages.description: Controlled vocabulary derived from semantic analysis of video content. Applied consistently across Obsidian vault via frontmatter tags.

design_decisions.decision: Claude as semantic filter between YouTube API and NotebookLM
design_decisions.rationale: YouTube search cannot evaluate research relevance. NotebookLM cannot select its own inputs. Claude reads unstructured metadata and applies natural-language research criteria. Pre-filtering protects NotebookLM retrieval quality.

design_decisions.decision: NotebookLM in critical path for research (Stages 3-5)
design_decisions.rationale: Provides managed RAG with persistent semantic index, cross-source synthesis, and grounded citation. Pro tier (300 sources/notebook) handles target corpus of ~200 videos. Building equivalent capabilities from scratch requires significant engineering investment in embeddings, vector store, and retrieval infrastructure.
design_decisions.reassess_if: NotebookLM API access becomes unreliable, source limits prove insufficient, or retrieval quality degrades at scale.

design_decisions.decision: MCP path for NotebookLM automation (over Python CLI)
design_decisions.rationale: Tighter agentic feedback loop. Claude reacts to failures in real time. More natural for conversational research sessions.
design_decisions.fallback: notebooklm-py CLI scripts. Produces standalone, inspectable, version-controllable artifacts.
design_decisions.reassess_if: MCP server breaks due to undocumented API changes, auth token management becomes burdensome, or pipeline needs to run independently of Claude Code.

design_decisions.decision: Corpus editorial policy as discrete subtask (1A)
design_decisions.rationale: Filtering prompt is the highest-leverage design decision. Errors silently degrade all downstream stages. Requires its own iteration cycle separate from pipeline engineering.

implementation_plan.current_status: Stages 1-3 architecturally designed. No implementation begun.
sequence: Set up Google Cloud project, enable YouTube Data API v3, generate API key
Install notebooklm-mcp-cli, authenticate, verify Pro tier access
Implement Stage 1: YouTube search wrapper (API client, pagination, metadata normalization)
Design Subtask 1A: Corpus editorial policy prompt template
Implement Stage 1: Semantic filtering (Claude evaluates metadata against criteria)
Implement Stage 2: JSON staging (write checkpoint file with schema validation)
Test Stages 1-2 end-to-end with real YouTube search on a test topic
Implement Stage 3: NotebookLM ingestion via MCP (create notebook, batch-add URLs, log results)
Validate full Stage 1-3 pipeline on a real research topic
Design and implement Stages 4-7 (not yet specified)
implementation_plan.constraint: Subtask 1A (editorial policy prompt) must be completed before running pipeline against real data. It can be designed in parallel with Stage 1 API implementation.

engineering_philosophy: Incremental, reversible steps. No monolithic builds.
Smallest safe step. One-sentence check before multi-step plans.
Pragmatic over clever. Simple, readable, maintainable code.
Debug root causes, not symptoms. No workarounds.
Match the style of surrounding code. Local consistency trumps external standards.
project_constraints.naming: Domain story, not implementation story. Registry not ToolRegistryManager. No temporal markers.
project_constraints.testing: Test logic that could regress. Test-first when expected behavior is clear. Tests cover real logic, not mocked behavior.

directories.config/: API keys (gitignored), research criteria prompt templates
directories.src/stage/: Stage 2: JSON staging and schema validation
